source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And thou shalt set upon the table loaves of proposition in my sight always.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thou shalt make also a candlestick of beaten work, of the finest gold, the shaft thereof, and the branches, the cups, and the bowls, and the lilies going forth from it.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Six branches shall come out of the sides, three out of one side, and three out of the other.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Three cups as it were nuts to every branch, and a bowl withal, and a lily: and three cups likewise of the fashion of nuts in the other branch, and a bowl withal, and a lily. Such shall be the work of the six branches, that are to come out from the shaft:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And in the candlestick itself shall be four cups in the manner of a nut, and at every one bowls and lilies.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Bowls under two branches in three places, which together make six, coming forth out of one shaft.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And both the bowls and the branches shall be of the same beaten work of the purest gold.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thou shalt make also seven lamps, and shalt set them upon the candlestick, to give light over against.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The snuffers also, and where the snuffings shall be put out, shall be made of the purest gold.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The whole weight of the candlestick, with all the furniture thereof, shall be a talent of the purest gold.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Look, and make it according to the pattern that was shewn thee in the mount.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And thou shalt make the tabernacle in this manner: Thou shalt make ten curtains of fine twisted linen, and violet and purple, and scarlet twice dyed, diversified with embroidery.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The length of one curtain shall be twenty-eight cubits; the breadth shall be four cubits. All the curtains shall be of one measure.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Five curtains shall be joined one to another, and the other five shall be coupled together in like manner.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thou shalt make loops of violet in the sides and tops of the curtains, that they may be joined one to another.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Every curtain shall have fifty loops on both sides, so set on, that one loop may be against another loop, and one may be fitted to the other.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thou shalt make also fifty rings of gold, wherewith the veils of the curtains are to be joined, that it may be made one tabernacle.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thou shalt make also eleven curtains of goats’ hair, to cover the top of the tabernacle.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The length of one hair-curtain shall be thirty cubits; and the breadth, four: the measure of all the curtains shall be equal.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Five of which thou shalt couple by themselves, and the six others thou shalt couple one to another, so as to double the sixth curtain in the front of the roof.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thou shalt make also fifty loops in the edge of one curtain, that it may be joined with the other: and fifty loops in the edge of the other curtain, that it may be coupled with its fellow.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thou shalt make also fifty buckles of brass, wherewith the loops may be joined, that of all there may be made one covering.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And that which shall remain of the curtains, that are prepared for the roof, to wit, one curtain that is over and above, with the half thereof thou shalt cover the back parts of the tabernacle.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And there shall hang down a cubit on the one side, and another on the other side, which is over and above in the length of the curtains, fencing both sides of the tabernacle.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thou shalt make also another cover to the roof of rams’ skins dyed red: and over that again another cover of violet coloured skins.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thou shalt make also the boards of the tabernacle standing upright of setim wood.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Let every one of them be ten cubits in length, and in breadth one cubit and a half.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: In the sides of the boards shall be made two mortises, whereby one board may be joined to another board: and after this manner shall all the boards be prepared.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Of which twenty shall be in the south side southward.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For which thou shalt cast forty sockets of silver, that under every board may be put two sockets at the two corners.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: In the second side also of the tabernacle that looketh to the north, there shall be twenty boards,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Having forty sockets of silver, two sockets shall be put under each board.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But on the west side of the tabernacle thou shalt make six boards.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And again other two which shall be erected in the corners at the back of the tabernacle.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And they shall be joined together from beneath unto the top, and one joint shall hold them all. The like joining shall be observed for the two boards also that are to be put in the corners.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And they shall be in all eight boards, and their silver sockets sixteen, reckoning two sockets for each board.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thou shalt make also five bars of setim wood, to hold together the boards on one side of the tabernacle.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And five others on the other side, and as many at the west side:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And they shall be put along by the midst of the boards, from one end to the other.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The boards also themselves thou shalt overlay with gold, and shalt cast rings of gold to be set upon them, for places for the bars to hold together the boardwork: which bars thou shalt cover with plates of gold.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And thou shalt rear up the tabernacle according to the pattern that was shewn thee in the mount.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thou shalt make also a veil of violet, and purple, and scarlet twice dyed, and fine twisted linen, wrought with embroidered work and goodly variety:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And thou shalt hang it up before four pillars of setim wood, which themselves also shall be overlaid with gold, and shall have heads of gold, but sockets of silver.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the veil shall be hanged on with rings, and within it thou shalt put the ark of the testimony, and the sanctuary and the holy of the holies shall be divided with it.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And thou shalt set the propitiatory upon the ark of the testimony, in the holy of holies.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the table without the veil, and over against the table the candlestick in the south side of the tabernacle: for the table shall stand in the north side.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thou shalt make also a hanging in the entrance of the tabernacle of violet, and purple, and scarlet twice dyed, and fine twisted linen with embroidered work.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And thou shalt overlay with gold five pillars of setim wood, before which the hanging shall be drawn: their heads shall be of gold, and the sockets of brass.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thou shalt make also an altar of setim wood, which shall be five cubits long, and as many broad, that is four square, and three cubits high.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And there shall be horns at the four corners of the same: and thou shalt cover it with brass.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And thou shalt make for the uses thereof pans to receive the ashes, and tongs and fleshhooks, and firepans: all its vessels thou shalt make of brass.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And a grate of brass in manner of a net; at the four corners of which, shall be four rings of brass,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Which thou shalt put under the hearth of the altar: and the grate shall be even to the midst of the altar.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thou shalt make also two bars for the altar, of setim wood, which thou shalt cover with plates of brass:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And thou shalt draw them through rings, and they shall be on both sides of the altar to carry it.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thou shalt not make it solid, but empty and hollow in the inside, as it was shewn thee in the mount.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thou shalt make also the court of the tabernacle, in the south side whereof southward there shall be hangings of fine twisted linen of a hundred cubits long for one side.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And twenty pillars with as many sockets of brass, the heads of which, with their engraving, shall be of silver.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: In like manner also on the north side there shall be hangings of a hundred cubits long, twenty pillars, and as many sockets of brass, and their heads with their engraving of silver.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But in the breadth of the court, that looketh to the west, there shall be hangings of fifty cubits, and ten pillars, and as many sockets.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: In that breadth also of the court, which looketh to the east, there shall be fifty cubits.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: In which there shall be for one side, hangings of fifteen cubits, and three pillars, and as many sockets.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And in the other side, there shall be hangings of fifteen cubits, with three pillars, and as many sockets.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And in the entrance of the court there shall be made a hanging of twenty cubits of violet and purple, and scarlet twice dyed, and fine twisted linen, with embroidered work: it shall have four pillars, with as many sockets.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: All the pillars of the court round about shall be garnished with plates of silver, silver heads, and sockets of brass.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: In length the court shall take up a hundred cubits, in breadth fifty, the height shall be of five cubits, and it shall be made of fine twisted linen, and shall have sockets of brass.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: All the vessels of the tabernacle for all uses and ceremonies, and the pins both of it and of the court, thou shalt make of brass.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Command the children of Israel that they bring thee the purest oil of the olives, and beaten with a pestle: that a lamp may burn always,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: In the tabernacle of the testimony, without the veil that hangs before the testimony. And Aaron and his sons shall order it, that it may give light before the Lord until the morning. It shall be a perpetual observance throughout their successions among the children of Israel.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Take unto thee also Aaron thy brother with his sons, from among the children of Israel, that they may minister to me in the priest’s office: Aaron, Nadab, and Abiu, Eleazar, and Ithamar.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And thou shalt make a holy vesture for Aaron, thy brother, for glory and for beauty.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And thou shalt speak to all the wise of heart, whom I have filled with the spirit of wisdom, that they may make Aaron’s vestments, in which he being consecrated, may minister to me.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And these shall be the vestments that they shall make: A rational and an ephod, a tunic and a strait linen garment, a mitre and a girdle. They shall make the holy vestments for thy brother Aaron and his sons, that they may do the office of priesthood unto me.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And they shall take gold, and violet, and purple, and scarlet twice dyed, and fine linen.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And they shall make the ephod of gold, and violet, and purple, and scarlet twice dyed, and fine twisted linen, embroidered with divers colours.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: It shall have the two edges joined in the top on both sides, that they may be closed together.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The very workmanship also, and all the variety of the work, shall be of gold, and violet, and purple, and scarlet twice dyed, and fine twisted linen.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And thou shalt take two onyx stones, and shalt grave on them the names of the children of Israel:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Six names on one stone, and the other six on the other, according to the order of their birth.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: With the work of an engraver, and the graving of a jeweller, thou shalt engrave them with the names of the children of Israel, set in gold and compassed about:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And thou shalt put them in both sides of the ephod, a memorial for the children of Israel. And Aaron shall bear their names before the Lord upon both shoulders, for a remembrance.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thou shalt make also hooks of gold.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And two little chains of the purest gold, linked one to another, which thou shalt put into the hooks.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And thou shalt make the rational of judgment with embroidered work of divers colours, according to the workmanship of the ephod, of gold, violet, and purple, and scarlet twice dyed, and fine twisted linen.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: It shall be four square and doubled: it shall be the measure of a span both in length and in breadth.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And thou shalt set in it four rows of stones . In the first row shall be a sardius stone, and a topaz, and an emerald:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: In the second a carbuncle, a sapphire, and a jasper:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: In the third a ligurius, an agate, and an amethyst:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: In the fourth a chrysolite, an onyx, and a beryl. They shall be set in gold by their rows.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And they shall have the names of the children of Israel: with twelve names shall they be engraved, each stone with the name of one according to the twelve tribes.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And thou shalt make on the rational chains, linked one to another, of the purest gold:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And two rings of gold, which thou shalt put in the two ends at the top of the rational.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the golden chains thou shalt join to the rings, that are in the ends thereof.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the ends of the chains themselves, thou shalt join together with two hooks, on both sides of the ephod, which is towards the rational.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thou shalt make also two rings of gold, which thou shalt put in the top parts of the rational, in the borders that are over against the ephod, and look towards the back parts thereof.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Moreover also other two rings of gold, which are to be set on each side of the ephod beneath, that looketh towards the nether joining, that the rational may be fitted with the ephod,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And may be fastened by the rings thereof unto the rings of the ephod with a violet fillet, that the joining artificially wrought may continue, and the rational and the ephod may not be loosed one from the other.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Aaron shall bear the names of the children of Israel in the rational of judgment upon his breast, when he shall enter into the sanctuary, a memorial before the Lord for ever.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And thou shalt put in the rational of judgment doctrine and truth, which shall be on Aaron’s breast, when he shall go in before the Lord: and he shall bear the judgment of the children of Israel on his breast, in the sight of the Lord always.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And thou shalt make the tunic of the ephod all of violet,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: In the midst whereof above shall be a hole for the head, and a border round about it woven, as is wont to be made in the outmost parts of garments, that it may not easily be broken.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And beneath at the feet of the same tunic, round about, thou shalt make as it were pomegranates, of violet, and purple, and scarlet twice dyed, with little bells set between:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: So that there shall be a golden bell and a pomegranate, and again another golden bell and a pomegranate.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Aaron shall be vested with it in the office of his ministry, that the sound may be heard, when he goeth in and cometh out of the sanctuary, in the sight of the Lord, and that he may not die.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thou shalt make also a plate of the purest gold: wherein thou shalt grave with engraver’s work, Holy to the Lord.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And thou shalt tie it with a violet fillet, and it shall be upon the mitre,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Hanging over the forehead of the high priest. And Aaron shall bear the iniquities of those things, which the children of Israel have offered and sanctified, in all their gifts and offerings. And the plate shall be always on his forehead, that the Lord may be well pleased with them.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And thou shalt gird the tunic with fine linen, and thou shalt make a fine linen mitre, and a girdle of embroidered work.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Moreover, for the sons of Aaron thou shalt prepare linen tunics, and girdles and mitres for glory and beauty:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And with all these things thou shalt vest Aaron thy brother, and his sons with him. And thou shalt consecrate the hands of them all, and shalt sanctify them, that they may do the office of priesthood unto me.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thou shalt make also linen breeches, to cover the flesh of their nakedness, from the reins to the thighs:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Aaron and his sons shall use them when they shall go into the tabernacle of the testimony, or when they approach to the altar to minister in the sanctuary, lest being guilty of iniquity they die. It shall be a law for ever to Aaron, and to his seed after him.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And thou shalt also do this, that they may be consecrated to me in priesthood. Take a calf from the herd, and two rams without blemish,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And unleavened bread, and a cake without leaven, tempered with oil, wafers also unleavened, anointed with oil: thou shalt make them all of wheaten flour.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And thou shalt put them in a basket, and offer them: and the calf and the two rams.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And thou shalt bring Aaron and his sons to the door of the tabernacle of the testimony. And when thou hast washed the father and his sons with water,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thou shalt clothe Aaron with his vestments, that is, with the linen garment and the tunic, and the ephod and the rational, which thou shalt gird with the girdle.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And thou shalt put the mitre upon his head, and the holy plate upon the mitre,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And thou shalt pour the oil of unction upon his head: and by this rite shall he be consecrated.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thou shalt bring his sons also, and shalt put on them the linen tunics, and gird them with a girdle:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: To wit, Aaron and his children, and thou shalt put mitres upon them; and they shall be priests to me by a perpetual ordinance. After thou shalt have consecrated their hands,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thou shalt present also the calf before the tabernacle of the testimony. And Aaron and his sons shall lay their hands upon his head,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And thou shalt kill him in the sight of the Lord, beside the door of the tabernacle of the testimony.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And taking some of the blood of the calf, thou shalt put it upon the horns of the altar with thy finger, and the rest of the blood thou shalt pour at the bottom thereof.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thou shalt take also all the fat that covereth the entrails, and the caul of the liver, and the two kidneys, and the fat that is upon them, and shalt offer a burnt offering upon the altar:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But the flesh of the calf, and the hide and the dung, thou shalt burn abroad, without the camp, because it is for sin.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thou shalt take also one ram, upon the head whereof Aaron and his sons shall lay their hands.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And when thou hast killed him, thou shalt take of the blood thereof, and pour round about the altar.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And thou shalt cut the ram in pieces, and having washed his entrails and feet, thou shalt put them upon the flesh that is cut in pieces, and upon his head.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And thou shalt offer the whole ram for a burnt offering upon the altar: it is an oblation to the Lord, a most sweet savour of the victim of the Lord.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thou shalt take also the other ram, upon whose head Aaron and his sons shall lay their hands.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And when thou hast sacrificed him, thou shalt take of his blood, and put upon the tip of the right ear of Aaron and of his sons, and upon the thumbs and great toes of their right hand and foot, and thou shalt pour the blood upon the altar round about.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And when thou hast taken of the blood that is upon the altar, and of the oil of unction, thou shalt sprinkle Aaron and his vesture, his sons and their vestments. And after they and their vestments are consecrated,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thou shalt take the fat of the ram, and the rump, and the fat that covereth the lungs, and the caul of the liver, and the two kidneys, and the fat that is upon them, and the right shoulder, because it is the ram of consecration:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And one roll of bread, a cake tempered with oil, a wafer out of the basket of unleavened bread, which is set in the sight of the Lord:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And thou shalt put all upon the hands of Aaron and of his sons, and shalt sanctify them elevating before the Lord.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And thou shalt take all from their hands; and shalt burn them upon the altar for a holocaust, a most sweet savour in the sight of the Lord, because it is his oblation.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thou shalt take also the breast of the ram, wherewith Aaron was consecrated, and elevating it thou shalt sanctify it before the Lord, and it shall fall to thy share.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And thou shalt sanctify both the consecrated breast, and the shoulder that thou didst separate of the ram,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Wherewith Aaron was consecrated and his sons, and they shall fall to Aaron’s share, and his sons’, by a perpetual right from the children of Israel: because they are the choicest and the beginnings of their peace victims which they offer to the Lord.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the holy vesture, which Aaron shall use, his sons shall have after him, that they may be anointed, and their hands consecrated in it.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: He of his sons that shall be appointed high priest in his stead, and that shall enter into the tabernacle of the testimony to minister in the sanctuary, shall wear it seven days.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And thou shalt take the ram of the consecration, and shalt boil the flesh thereof in the holy place:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Aaron and his sons shall eat it. The loaves also, that are in the basket, they shall eat in the entry of the tabernacle of the testimony,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: That it may be an atoning sacrifice, and the hands of the offerers may be sanctified. A stranger shall not eat of them, because they are holy.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And if there remain of the consecrated flesh, or of the bread, till the morning, thou shalt burn the remainder with fire: they shall not be eaten, because they are sanctified.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: All that I have commanded thee, thou shalt do unto Aaron and his sons. Seven days shalt thou consecrate their hands:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And thou shalt offer a calf for sin every day for expiation. And thou shalt cleanse the altar when thou hast offered the victim of expiation, and shalt anoint it to sanctify it.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Seven days shalt thou expiate the altar and sanctify it, and it shall be most holy. Every one, that shall touch it, shall be holy.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: This is what thou shalt sacrifice upon the altar: Two lambs of a year old every day continually,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: One lamb in the morning, and another in the evening.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: With one lamb a tenth part of flour tempered with beaten oil, of the fourth part of a hin, and wine for libation of the same measure.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the other lamb thou shalt offer in the evening, according to the rite of the morning oblation, and according to what we have said, for a savour of sweetness:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: It is a sacrifice to the Lord, by perpetual oblation unto your generations, at the door of the tabernacle of the testimony before the Lord, where I will appoint to speak unto thee.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And there will I command the children of Israel, and the altar shall be sanctified by my glory.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: I will sanctify also the tabernacle of the testimony with the altar, and Aaron with his sons, to do the office of priesthood unto me.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And I will dwell in the midst of the children of Israel, and will be their God:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And they shall know that I am the Lord their God, who have brought them out of the land of Egypt, that I might abide among them, I the Lord their God.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thou shalt make also an altar to burn incense, of setim wood.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: It shall be a cubit in length, and another in breadth, that is, four square, and two in height. Horns shall go out of the same.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And thou shalt overlay it with the purest gold, as well the grate thereof, as the walls round about, and the horns. And thou shalt make to it a crown of gold round about,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And two golden rings under the crown on either side, that the bars may be put into them, and the altar be carried.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And thou shalt make the bars also of setim wood, and shalt overlay them with gold.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And thou shalt set the altar over against the veil, that hangeth before the ark of the testimony before the propitiatory wherewith the testimony is covered, where I will speak to thee.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Aaron shall burn sweet smelling incense upon it in the morning. When he shall dress the lamps, he shall burn it:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And when he shall place them in the evening, he shall burn an everlasting incense before the Lord throughout your generations.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: You shall not offer upon it incense of another composition, nor oblation, and victim, neither shall you offer libations.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Aaron shall pray upon the horns thereof once a year, with the blood of that which was offered for sin; and shall make atonement upon it in your generations. It shall be most holy to the Lord.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the Lord spoke to Moses, saying:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: When thou shalt take the sum of the children of Israel, according to their number, every one of them shall give a price for their souls to the Lord, and there shall be no scourge among them, when they shall be reckoned.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And this shall every one give that passeth at the naming, half a sicle according to the standard of the temple. A sicle hath twenty obols. Half a sicle shall be offered to the Lord.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: He that is counted in the number from twenty years and upwards, shall give the price.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The rich man shall not add to half a sicle, and the poor man shall diminish nothing.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the money received, which was contributed by the children of Israel, thou shalt deliver unto the uses of the tabernacle of the testimony, that it may be a memorial of them before the Lord, and he may be merciful to their souls.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the Lord spoke to Moses, saying:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thou shalt make also a brazen laver with its foot to wash in: and thou shalt set it between the tabernacle of the testimony and the altar. And water being put into it:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Aaron and his sons shall wash their hands and feet in it:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: When they are going into the tabernacle of the testimony, and when they are to come to the altar, to offer on it incense to the Lord,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Lest perhaps they die. It shall be an everlasting law to him, and to his seed by successions.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the Lord spoke to Moses,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Saying: Take spices, of principal and chosen myrrh five hundred sicles, and of cinnamon half so much; that is, two hundred and fifty sicles, of calamus in like manner two hundred and fifty,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And of cassia five hundred sicles by the weight of the sanctuary, of oil of olives the measure hin:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And thou shalt make the holy oil of unction, an ointment compounded after the art of the perfumer,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And therewith thou shalt anoint the tabernacle of the testimony, and the ark of the testament,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the table with the vessels thereof, the candlestick and furniture thereof, the altars of incense,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And of holocaust, and all the furniture that belongeth to the service of them.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And thou shalt sanctify all, and they shall be most holy: he that shall touch them shall be sanctified.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thou shalt anoint Aaron and his sons, and shalt sanctify them, that they may do the office of priesthood unto me.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And thou shalt say to the children of Israel: This oil of unction shall be holy unto me throughout your generations.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The flesh of man shall not be anointed therewith, and you shall make none other of the same composition, because it is sanctified, and shall be holy unto you.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: What man soever shall compound such, and shall give thereof to a stranger, he shall be cut off from his people.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the Lord said to Moses: Take unto thee spices, stacte, and onycha, galbanum of sweet savour, and the clearest frankincense, all shall be of equal weight.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And thou shalt make incense compounded by the work of the perfumer, well tempered together, and pure, and most worthy of sanctification.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And when thou hast beaten all into very small powder, thou shalt set of it before the tabernacle of the testimony, in the place where I will appear to thee. Most holy shall this incense be unto you.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: You shall not make such a composition for your own uses, because it is holy to the Lord.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: What man soever shall make the like, to enjoy the smell thereof, he shall perish out of his people.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the Lord spoke to Moses, saying:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Behold, I have called by name Beseleel the son of Uri, the son of Hur, of the tribe of Juda,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And I have filled him with the spirit of God, with wisdom and understanding, and knowledge in all manner of work,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: To devise whatsoever may be artificially made of gold, and silver, and brass,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Of marble, and precious stones, and variety of wood.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And I have given him for his companion Ooliab, the son of Achisamech, of the tribe of Dan. And I have put wisdom in the heart of every skilful man, that they may make all things which I have commanded thee,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The tabernacle of the covenant, and the ark of the testimony, and the propitiatory, that is over it, and all the vessels of the tabernacle,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the table and the vessels thereof, the most pure candlestick with the vessels thereof, and the altars of incense,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And of holocaust, and all their vessels, the laver with its foot,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The holy vestments in the ministry for Aaron the priest, and for his sons, that they may execute their office, about the sacred things:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The oil of unction, and the incense of spices in the sanctuary, all things which I have commanded thee, shall they make.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the Lord spoke to Moses, saying:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Speak to the children of Israel, and thou shalt say to them: See that you keep my sabbath; because it is a sign between me and you in your generations that you may know that I am the Lord, who sanctify you.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: keep you my sabbath: for it is holy unto you: he that shall profane it, shall be put to death: he that shall do any work in it, his soul shall perish out of the midst of his people.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Six days shall you do work: in the seventh day is the sabbath, the rest holy to the Lord. Every one that shall do any work on this day, shall die.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Let the children of Israel keep the sabbath, and celebrate it in their generations.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: It is an everlasting covenant, and a perpetual sign between me and the children of Israel. For in six days the Lord made heaven and earth, and in the seventh he ceased from work.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the Lord, when he had ended these words in Mount Sinai, gave to Moses two stone tables of testimony, written with the finger of God.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the people seeing that Moses delayed to come down from the mount, gathering together against Aaron, said: Arise, make us gods, that may go before us: For as to this Moses, the man that brought us out of the land of Egypt, we know not what has befallen him.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Aaron said to them: Take the golden earrings from the ears of your wives, and your sons and daughters, and bring them to me.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the people did what he had commanded, bringing the earrings to Aaron.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And when he had received them, he fashioned them by founders’ work, and made of them a molten calf. And they said: These are thy gods, O Israel, that have brought thee out of the land of Egypt.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And when Aaron saw this, he built an altar before it, and made proclamation by a crier’s voice, saying To morrow is the solemnity of the Lord.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And rising in the morning, they offered holocausts, and peace victims, and the people sat down to eat and drink, and they rose up to play.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the Lord spoke to Moses, saying: Go, get thee down: thy people, which thou hast brought out of the land of Egypt, hath sinned.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: They have quickly strayed from the way which thou didst shew them: and they have made to themselves a molten calf, and have adored it, and sacrificing victims to it, have said: These are thy gods, O Israel, that have brought thee out of the land of Egypt.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And again the Lord said to Moses: I see that this people is stiffnecked:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Let me alone, that my wrath may be kindled against them, and that I may destroy them, and I will make of thee a great nation.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But Moses besought the Lord his God, saying: Why, O Lord, is thy indignation enkindled against thy people, whom thou hast brought out of the land of Egypt, with great power, and with a mighty hand?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Let not the Egyptians say, I beseech thee: He craftily brought them out, that he might kill them in the mountains, and destroy them from the earth: let thy anger cease, and be appeased upon the wickedness of thy people.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Remember Abraham, Isaac, and Israel, thy servants, to whom thou sworest by thy own self, saying: I will multiply your seed as the stars of heaven: and this whole land that I have spoken of, I will give to your seed, and you shall possess it for ever:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the Lord was appeased from doing the evil which he had spoken against his people.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Moses returned from the mount, carrying the two tables of the testimony in his hand, written on both sides,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And made by the work of God; the writing also of God was graven in the tables.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Josue hearing the noise of the people shouting, said to Moses: The noise of battle is heard in the camp.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But he answered: It is not the cry of men encouraging to fight, nor the shout of men compelling to flee: but I hear the voice of singers.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And when he came nigh to the camp, he saw the calf, and the dances: and being very angry, he threw the tables out of his hand, and broke them at the foot of the mount:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And laying hold of the calf which they had made, he burnt it, and beat it to powder, which he strewed into water, and gave thereof to the children of Israel to drink.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he said to Aaron: What has this people done to thee, that thou shouldst bring upon them a most heinous sin?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he answered him: Let not my lord be offended; for thou knowest this people, that they are prone to evil.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: They said to me: make us gods, that may go before us; for as to this Moses, who brought us forth out of the land of Egypt, we know not what is befallen him.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And I said to them: Which of you hath any gold? and they took and brought it to me; and I cast it into the fire, and this calf came out.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And when Moses saw that the people were naked, (for Aaron had stripped them by occasion of the shame of the filth, and had set them naked among their enemies)

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Then standing in the gate of the camp, he said: If any man be on the Lord’s side, let him join with me. And all the sons of Levi gathered themselves together unto him:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he said to them: Thus saith the Lord God of Israel: Put every man his sword upon his thigh: go, and return from gate to gate through the midst of the camp, and let every man kill his brother, and friend, and neighbour.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the sons of Levi did according to the words of Moses, and there were slain that day about three and twenty thousand men.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Moses said: You have consecrated your hands this day to the Lord, every man in his son and in his brother, that a blessing may be given to you.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And when the next day was come, Moses spoke to the people: You have sinned a very great sin: I will go up to the Lord, if by any means I may be able to entreat him for your crime.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And returning to the Lord, he said: I beseech thee: this people hath sinned a heinous sin, and they have made to themselves gods of gold: either forgive them this trespass,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Or if thou do not, strike me out of the book that thou hast written.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the Lord answered him: He that hath sinned against me, him will I strike out of my book:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But go thou, and lead this people whither I have told thee: my angel shall go before thee. And I in the day of revenge will visit this sin also of theirs.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The Lord therefore struck the people for the guilt, on occasion of the calf which Aaron had made.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the Lord spoke to Moses, saying: Go, get thee up from this place, thou and thy people which thou hast brought out of the land of Egypt, into the land concerning which I swore to Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob, saying: To thy seed I will give it:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And I will send an angel before thee, that I may cast out the Chanaanite, and the Amorrhite, and the Hethite, and the Pherezite, and the Hevite, and the Jebusite,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: That thou mayst enter into the land that floweth with milk and honey. For I will not go up with thee, because thou art a stiffnecked people; lest I destroy thee in the way.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the people hearing these very bad tidings, mourned: and no man put on his ornaments according to custom.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the Lord said to Moses: Say to the children of Israel: Thou art a stiffnecked people, once I shall come up in the midst of thee, and shall destroy thee. Now presently lay aside thy ornaments, that I may know what to do to thee.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: So the children of Israel laid aside their ornaments by Mount Horeb.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Moses also taking the tabernacle, pitched it without the camp afar off, and called the name thereof, The tabernacle of the covenant. And all the people, that had any question, went forth to the tabernacle of the covenant, without the camp.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And when Moses went forth to the tabernacle, all the people rose up, and every one stood in the door of his pavilion, and they beheld the back of Moses, till he went into the tabernacle.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And when he was gone into the tabernacle of the covenant, the pillar of the cloud came down, and stood at the door, and he spoke with Moses.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And all saw that the pillar of the cloud stood at the door of the tabernacle. And they stood and worshipped at the doors of their tent.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the Lord spoke to Moses face to face, as a man is wont to speak to his friend. And when he returned into the camp, his servant Josue, the son of Nun, a young man, departed not from the tabernacle.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Moses said to the Lord: Thou commandest me to lead forth this people; and thou dost not let me know whom thou wilt send with me, especially whereas thou hast said: I know thee by name, and thou hast found favour in my sight.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: If therefore I have found favour in thy sight, shew me thy face, that I may know thee, and may find grace before thy eyes: look upon thy people this nation.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the Lord said: My face shall go before thee, and I will give thee rest.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Moses said: If thou thyself dost not go before, bring us not out of this place.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For how shall we be able to know, I and thy people, that we have found grace in thy sight, unless thou walk with us, that we may be glorified by all people that dwell upon the earth?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the Lord said to Moses: This word also, which thou hast spoken, will I do; for thou hast found grace before me, and thee I have known by name.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he said: Shew me thy glory.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: He answered: I will shew thee all good, and I will proclaim in the name of the Lord before thee: and I will have mercy on whom I will, and I will be merciful to whom it shall please me.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And again he said: Thou canst not see my face: for man shall not see me, and live.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And again he said: Behold there is a place with me, and thou shalt stand upon the rock.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And when my glory shall pass, I will set thee in a hole of the rock, and protect thee with my right hand till I pass:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And I will take away my hand, and thou shalt see my back parts: but my face thou canst not see.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And after this he said: Hew thee two tables of stone like unto the former, and I will write upon them the words, which were in the tables, which thou brokest.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Be ready in the morning, that thou mayst forthwith go up into Mount Sinai, and thou shalt stand with me upon the top of the mount.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Let no man go up with thee, and let not any man be seen throughout all the mount; neither let the oxen nor the sheep feed over against it.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Then he cut out two tables of stone, such as had been before; and rising very early he went up into the Mount Sinai, as the Lord had commanded him, carrying with him the tables.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And when the Lord was come down in a cloud, Moses stood with him, calling upon the name of the Lord.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And when he passed before him, he said: O the Lord, the Lord God, merciful and gracious, patient and of much compassion, and true,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Who keepest mercy unto thousands: who takest away iniquity, and wickedness, and sin, and no man of himself is innocent before thee. Who renderest the iniquity of the fathers to the children, and to the grandchildren unto the third and fourth generation.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Moses making haste, bowed down prostrate unto the earth, and adoring,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Said: If I have found grace in thy sight, O Lord, I beseech thee that thou wilt go with us, (for it is a stiffnecked people) and take away our iniquities and sin, and possess us.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The Lord answered: I will make a covenant in the sight of all, I will do signs such as were never seen upon the earth, nor in any nations; that this people, in the midst of whom thou art, may see the terrible work of the Lord which I will do.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Observe all things which this day I command thee: I myself will drive out before thy face the Amorrhite, and the Chanaanite, and the Hethite, and the Pherezite, and the Hevite, and the Jebusite.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Beware thou never join in friendship with the inhabitants of that land, which may be thy ruin:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But destroy their altars, break their statues and cut down their groves:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Adore not any strange god. The Lord his name is jealous, he is a jealous God.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Make no covenant with the men of those countries; lest, when they have committed fornication with their gods, and have adored their idols, some one call thee to eat of the things sacrificed.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Neither shalt thou take of their daughters a wife for thy son, lest after they themselves have committed fornication, they make thy sons also to commit fornication with their gods.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thou shalt not make to thyself any molten gods.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thou shalt keep the feast of the unleavened bread. Seven days shalt thou eat unleavened bread, as I commanded thee in the time of the month of the new corn: for in the month of the spring time thou camest out from Egypt.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: All of the male kind that openeth the womb, shall be mine. Of all beasts; both of oxen and of sheep, it shall be mine.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The firstling of an ass thou shalt redeem with a sheep: but if thou wilt not give a price for it, it shall be slain. The firstborn of thy sons thou shalt redeem: neither shalt thou appear before me empty.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Six days shalt thou work, the seventh day thou shalt cease to plough and to reap.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thou shalt keep the feast of weeks with the firstfruits of the corn of thy wheat harvest, and the feast when the time of the year returneth that all things are laid in.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Three times in the year all thy males shall appear in the sight of the almighty Lord the God of Israel.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For when I shall have taken away the nations from thy face, and shall have enlarged thy borders, no man shall lie in wait against thy land when thou shalt go up, and appear in the sight of the Lord thy God thrice in a year.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thou shalt not offer the blood of my sacrifice upon leaven; neither shall there remain in the morning any thing of the victim of the solemnity of the Phase.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The first of the fruits of thy ground thou shalt offer in the house of the Lord thy God. Thou shalt not boil a kid in the milk of his dam.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the Lord said to Moses: Write thee these words, by which I have made a covenant both with thee and with Israel.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he was there with the Lord forty days and forty nights: he neither ate bread nor drank water, and he wrote upon the tables the ten words of the covenant.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And when Moses came down from the Mount Sinai, he held the two tables of the testimony, and he knew not that his face was horned from the conversation of the Lord.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Aaron and the children of Israel seeing the face of Moses horned, were afraid to come near.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And being called by him, they returned, both Aaron and the rulers of the congregation. And after that he spoke to them,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And all the children of Israel came to him: and he gave them in commandment all that he had heard of the Lord on Mount Sinai.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And having done speaking, he put a veil upon his face.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But when he went in to the Lord, and spoke with him, he took it away until he came forth, and then he spoke to the children of Israel all things that had been commanded him.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And they saw that the face of Moses when he came out was horned, but he covered his face again, if at any time he spoke to them.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And all the multitude of the children of Israel being gathered together, he said to them: These are the things which the Lord hath commanded to be done:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Six days you shall do work; the seventh day shall be holy unto you, the sabbath and the rest of the Lord: he that shall do any work on it, shall be put to death.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: You shall kindle no fire in any of your habitations on the sabbath day.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Moses said to all the assembly of the children of Israel: This is the word the Lord hath commanded, saying:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Set aside with you firstfruits to the Lord. Let every one that is willing and hath a ready heart, offer them to the Lord: gold, and silver, and brass,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Violet and purple, and scarlet twice dyed, and fine linen, goats’ hair,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And rams’ skins dyed red, and violet coloured skins, setim wood,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And oil to maintain lights, and to make ointment, and most sweet incense,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Onyx stones, and precious stones, for the adorning of the ephod and the rational.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Whosoever of you is wise, let him come, and make that which the Lord hath commanded:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: To wit, the tabernacle, and the roof thereof, and the cover, the rings, and the board-work with the bars, the pillars and the sockets:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The ark and the staves, the propitiatory, and the veil that is drawn before it:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The table with the bars and the vessels, and the loaves of proposition:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The candlestick to bear up the lights, the vessels thereof and the lamps, and the oil for the nourishing of fires:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The altar of incense, and the bars, and the oil of unction, and the incense of spices: the hanging at the door of the tabernacle:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The altar of holocaust, and its grate of brass, with the bars and vessels thereof: the laver and its foot:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The curtains of the court, with the pillars and the sockets, the hanging in the doors of the entry.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The pins of the tabernacle, and of the court, with their little cords:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The vestments that are to be used in the ministry of the sanctuary, the vesture of Aaron the high priest, and of his sons, to do the office of priesthood to me.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And all the multitude of the children of Israel going out from the presence of Moses,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Offered firstfruits to the Lord with a most ready and devout mind, to make the work of the tabernacle of the testimony. Whatever was necessary to the service and to the holy vestments,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Both men and women gave bracelets and earrings, rings and tablets: every vessel of gold was set aside to be offered to the Lord.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: If any man had violet, and purple, and scarlet twice dyed, fine linen and goats’ hair, ramskins dyed red, and violet coloured skins,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Metal of silver and brass, they offered it to the Lord, and setim wood for divers uses.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The skilful women also gave such things as they had spun, violet, purple, and scarlet, and fine linen,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And goats’ hair, giving all of their own accord.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But the princes offered onyx stones, and precious stones, for the ephod and the rational,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And spices and oil for the lights, and for the preparing of ointment, and to make the incense of most sweet savour.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: All, both men and women, with devout mind offered gifts, that the works might be done which the Lord had commanded by the hand of Moses. All the children of Israel dedicated voluntary offerings to the Lord.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Moses said to the children of Israel: Behold, the Lord hath called by name Beseleel, the son of Uri, the son of Hur, of the tribe of Juda,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And hath filled him with the spirit of God, with wisdom and understanding, and knowledge, and all learning,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: To devise and to work in gold and silver and brass,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And in engraving stones, and in carpenters’ work. Whatsoever can be devised artificially,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: He hath given in his heart: Ooliab also, the son of Achisamech, of the tribe of Dan:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Both of them hath he instructed with wisdom, to do carpenters’ work, and tapestry, and embroidery in blue and purple, and scarlet twice dyed, and fine linen, and to weave all things, and to invent all new things.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Beseleel therefore, and Ooliab, and every wise man, to whom the Lord gave wisdom and understanding, to know how to work artificially, made the things that are necessary for the uses of the sanctuary, and which the Lord commanded.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And when Moses had called them, and every skilful man, to whom the Lord had given wisdom, and such as of their own accord had offered themselves to the making of the work,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: He delivered all the offerings of the children of Israel unto them. And while they were earnest about the work, the people daily in the morning offered their vows.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Whereupon the workmen being constrained to come,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Said to Moses: The people offereth more than is necessary.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Moses therefore commanded proclamation to be made by the crier’s voice: Let neither man nor woman offer any more for the work of the sanctuary. And so they ceased from offering gifts,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Because the things that were offered did suffice, and were too much.
