source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And all the men that were wise of heart, to accomplish the work of the tabernacle, made ten curtains of twisted fine linen, and violet, and purple, and scarlet twice dyed, with varied work, and the art of embroidering:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The length of one curtain was twenty-eight cubits, and the breadth four: all the curtains were of the same size.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he joined five curtains, one to another, and the other five he coupled one to another.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: He made also loops of violet in the edge of one curtain on both sides, and in the edge of the other curtain in like manner,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: That the loops might meet one against another, and might be joined each with the other.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Whereupon also he cast fifty rings of gold, that might catch the loops of the curtains, and they might be made one tabernacle.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: He made also eleven curtains of goats’ hair, to cover the roof of the tabernacle:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: One curtain was thirty cubits long, and four cubits broad: all the curtains were of one measure.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Five of which he joined apart, and the other six apart.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he made fifty loops in the edge of one curtain, and fifty in the edge of another curtain, that they might be joined one to another.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And fifty buckles of brass wherewith the roof might be knit together, that of all the curtains there might be made one covering.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: He made also a cover for the tabernacle of rams’ skins dyed red; and another cover over that of violet skins.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: He made also the boards of the tabernacle of setim wood standing.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The length of one board was ten cubits; and the breadth was one cubit and a half.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: There were two mortises throughout every board, that one might be joined to the other. And in this manner he made for all the boards of the tabernacle.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Of which twenty were at the south side southward,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: With forty sockets of silver, two sockets were put under one board on the two sides of the corners, where the mortises of the sides end in the corners.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: At that side also of the tabernacle, that looketh towards the north, he made twenty boards,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: With forty sockets of silver, two sockets for every board.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But against the west, to wit, at that side of the tabernacle, which looketh to the sea, he made six boards,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And two others at each corner of the tabernacle behind:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Which were also joined from beneath unto the top, and went together into one joint. Thus he did on both sides at the corners:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: So there were in all eight boards, and they had sixteen sockets of silver, to wit, two sockets under every board.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: He made also bars of setim wood, five to hold together the boards of one side of the tabernacle,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And five others to join together the boards of the other side; and besides these, five other bars at the west side of the tabernacle towards the sea.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: He made also another bar, that might come by the midst of the boards from corner to corner.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the boards themselves he overlaid with gold casting for them sockets of silver. And their rings he made of gold, through which the bars might be drawn: and he covered the bars themselves with plates of gold.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: He made also a veil of violet, and purple, scarlet and fine twisted linen, varied and distinguished with embroidery:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And four pillars of setim wood, which with their heads he overlaid with gold, casting for them sockets of silver.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: He made also a hanging in the entry of the tabernacle of violet, purple, scarlet, and fine twisted linen, with the work of an embroiderer.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And five pillars with their heads, which he covered with gold, and their sockets he cast of brass.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Beseleel made also, the ark of setim wood: it was two cubits and a half in length, and a cubit and a half in breadth, and the height was of one cubit and a half: and he overlaid it with the purest gold within and without.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he made to it a crown of gold round about,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Casting four rings of gold at the four corners thereof: two rings in one side, and two in the other.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he made bars of setim wood, which he overlaid with gold,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he put them into the rings that were at the sides of the ark to carry it.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: He made also the propitiatory, that is, the oracle, of the purest gold, two cubits and a half in length, and a cubit and a half in breadth.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Two cherubims also of beaten gold, which he set on the two sides of the propitiatory:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: One cherub in the top of one side, and the other cherub in the top of the other side: two cherubims at the two ends of the propitiatory,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Spreading their wings, and covering the propitiatory, and looking one towards the other, and towards it.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: He made also the table of setim wood, in length two cubits, and in breadth one cubit, and in height it was a cubit and a half.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he overlaid it with the finest gold, and he made to it a golden ledge round about,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And to the ledge itself he made a polished crown of gold, of four fingers breadth, and upon the same another golden crown.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he cast four rings of gold, which he put in the four corners at each foot of the table,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Over against the crown: and he put the bars into them, that the table might be carried.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The bars also themselves he made of setim wood, and overlaid them with gold.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the vessels for the divers uses of the table, dishes, bowls, and cups, and censers of pure gold, wherein the libations are to be offered.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: He made also the candlestick of beaten work of the finest gold. From the shaft whereof its branches, its cups, and bowls, and lilies came out:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Six on the two sides: three branches on one side, and three on the other.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Three cups in manner of a nut on each branch, and bowls withal and lilies: and three cups of the fashion of a nut in another branch, and bowls withal and lilies. The work of the six branches, that went out from the shaft of the candlestick was equal.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And in the shaft itself were four cups after the manner of a nut, and bowls withal at every one, and lilies:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And bowls under two branches in three places, which together made six branches going out from one shaft.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: So both the bowls, and the branches were of the same, all beaten work of the purest gold.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: He made also the seven lamps with their snuffers, and the vessels where the snuffings were to be put out, of the purest gold.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The candlestick with all the vessels thereof weighed a talent of gold.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: He made also the altar of incense of setim wood, being a cubit on every side foursquare, and in height two cubits: from the corners of which went out horns.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he overlaid it with the purest gold, with its grate, and the sides, and the horns.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he made to it a crown of gold round about, and two golden rings under the crown at each side, that the bars might be put into them, and the altar be carried.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the bars themselves he made also of setim wood, and overlaid them with plates of gold.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: He compounded also the oil for the ointment of sanctification, and incense of the purest spices, according to the work of a perfumer.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: He made also the altar of holocaust of setim wood, five cubits square, and three in height:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The horns whereof went out from the corners, and he overlaid it with plates of brass.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And for the uses thereof, he prepared divers vessels of brass, cauldrons, tongs, fleshhooks, pothooks and firepans.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he made the grate thereof of brass, in manner of a net, and under it in the midst of the altar a hearth,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Casting four rings at the four ends of the net at the top, to put in bars to carry it:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he made the bars of setim wood, and overlaid them with plates of brass:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he drew them through the rings that stood out in the sides of the altar. And the altar itself was not solid, but hollow, of boards, and empty within.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: He made also the laver of brass, with the foot thereof, of the mirrors of the women that watched at the door of the tabernacle.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: He made also the court, in the south side whereof were hangings of fine twisted linen of a hundred cubits.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Twenty pillars of brass with their sockets, the heads of the pillars, and the whole graving of the work, of silver.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: In like manner at the north side the hangings, the pillars, and the sockets and heads of the pillars were of the same measure, and work and metal.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But on that side that looketh to the west, there were hangings of fifty cubits, ten pillars of brass with their sockets, and the heads of the pillars, and all the graving of the work, of silver.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Moreover, towards the east he prepared hangings of fifty cubits:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Fifteen cubits of which, were on one side with three pillars, and their sockets:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And on the other side (for between the two he made the entry of the tabernacle) there were hangings equally of fifteen cubits, and three pillars, and as many sockets.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: All the hangings of the court were woven with twisted linen.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The sockets of the pillars were of brass, and their heads with all their gravings of silver: and he overlaid the pillars of the court also with silver.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he made in the entry thereof an embroidered hanging of violet, purple, scarlet, and fine twisted linen, that was twenty cubits long, and five cubits high, according to the measure of all the hangings of the court.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the pillars in the entry were four, with sockets of brass, and their heads and gravings of silver.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The pins also of the tabernacle and of the court round about he made of brass.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: These are the instruments of the tabernacle of the testimony, which were counted according to the commandment of Moses, in the ceremonies of the Levites, by the hand of Ithamar, son of Aaron the priest:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Which Beseleel, the son of Uri, the son of Hur of the tribe of Juda, had made, as the Lord commanded by Moses.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Having for his companion Ooliab, the son of Achisamech, of the tribe of Dan: who also was an excellent artificer in wood, and worker in tapestry and embroidery in violet, purple, scarlet, and fine linen.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: All the gold that was spent in the work of the sanctuary, and that was offered in gifts, was nine and twenty talents, and seven hundred and thirty sicles according to the standard of the sanctuary.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And it was offered by them that went to be numbered, from twenty years old and upwards, of six hundred and three thousand five hundred and fifty men able to bear arms.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: There were moreover a hundred talents of silver, whereof were cast the sockets of the sanctuary, and of the entry where the veil hangeth.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: A hundred sockets were made of a hundred talents, one talent being reckoned for every socket.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And of the thousand seven hundred and seventy-five he made the heads of the pillars, which also he overlaid with silver.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And there were offered of brass also seventy-two thousand talents, and four hundred sicles besides,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Of which were cast the sockets in the entry of the tabernacle of the testimony, and the altar of brass with the grate thereof, and also the vessels that belong to the use thereof.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the sockets of the court as well round about as in the entry thereof, and the pins of the tabernacle, and of the court round about.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he made, of violet and purple, scarlet and fine linen, the vestments for Aaron to wear when he ministered in the holy places, as the Lord commanded Moses.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: So he made an ephod of gold, violet, and purple, and scarlet twice dyed, and fine twisted linen,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: With embroidered work, and he cut thin plates of gold, and drew them small into threads, that they might be twisted with the woof of the foresaid colours,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And two borders coupled one to the other in the top on either side,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And a girdle of the same colours, as the Lord had commanded Moses.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: He prepared also two onyx stones, fast set and closed in gold, and graven, by the art of a lapidary, with the names of the children of Israel:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he set them in the sides of the ephod, for a memorial of the children of Israel, as the Lord had commanded Moses.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: He made also a rational with embroidered work, according to the work of the ephod, of gold, violet, purple, and scarlet twice dyed, and fine twisted linen:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Foursquare, double, of the measure of a span.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he set four rows of precious stones in it. In the first row was a sardius, a topaz, an emerald.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: In the second, a carbuncle, a sapphire, and a jasper.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: In the third, a ligurius, an agate, and an amethyst.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: In the fourth, a chrysolite, an onyx, and a beryl, set and enclosed in gold by their rows.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the twelve stones, were engraved with the names of the twelve tribes of Israel, each one with its several name.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: They made also in the rational little chains, linked one to another, of the purest gold,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And two hooks, and as many rings of gold. And they set the rings on either side of the rational,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: On which rings the two golden chains should hang, which they put into the hooks that stood out in the corners of the ephod.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: These both before and behind so answered one another, that the ephod and the rational were bound together,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Being fastened to the girdle, and strongly coupled with rings, which a violet fillet joined, lest they should flag loose, and be moved one from the other, as the Lord commanded Moses.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: They made also the tunic of the ephod all of violet,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And a hole for the head in the upper part at the middle, and a woven border round about the hole:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And beneath at the feet pomegranates of violet, purple, scarlet, and fine twisted linen:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And little bells of the purest gold, which they put between the pomegranates at the bottom of the tunic round about:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: To wit, a bell of gold, and a pomegranate, wherewith the high priest went adorned, when he discharged his ministry, as the Lord had commanded Moses.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: They made also fine linen tunics with woven work for Aaron and his sons:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And mitres with their little crowns of fine linen:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And linen breeches of fine linen:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And a girdle of fine twisted linen, violet, purple, and scarlet twice dyed, of embroidery work, as the Lord had commanded Moses.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: They made also the plate of sacred veneration of the purest gold, and they wrote on it with the engraving of a lapidary: The Holy of the Lord:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And they fastened it to the mitre with a violet fillet, as the Lord had commanded Moses.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: So all the work of the tabernacle and of the roof of the testimony was finished: and the children of Israel did all things which the Lord had commanded Moses.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And they offered the tabernacle, and the roof, and the whole furniture, the rings, the boards, the bars, the pillars and their sockets,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The cover of rams’ skins dyed red, and the other cover of violet skins,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The veil, the ark, the bars, the propitiatory,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The table, with the vessels thereof, and the loaves of proposition:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The candlestick, the lamps, and the furniture of them, with the oil:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The altar of gold, and the ointment, and the incense of spices:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the hanging in the entry of the tabernacle:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The altar of brass, the grate, the bars, and all the vessels thereof: the laver, with the foot thereof: the hangings of the court, and the pillars, with their sockets:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The hanging in the entry of the court, and the little cords, and the pins thereof. Nothing was wanting of the vessels, that were commanded to be made for the ministry of the tabernacle, and for the roof of the covenant.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The vestments also, which the priests, to wit, Aaron and his sons, use in the sanctuary,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The children of Israel offered, as the Lord had commanded.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And when Moses saw all things finished, he blessed them.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the Lord spoke to Moses, saying:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The first month, the first day of the month, thou shalt set up the tabernacle of the testimony,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And shalt put the ark in it, and shalt let down the veil before it:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And thou shalt bring in the table, and set upon it the things that are commanded according to the rite. The candlestick shall stand with its lamps,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the altar of gold, whereon the incense is burnt before the ark of the testimony. Thou shalt put the hanging in the entry of the tabernacle,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And before it the altar of holocaust.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The laver between the altar and the tabernacle, and thou shalt fill it with water.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And thou shalt encompass the court with hangings, and the entry thereof.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And thou shalt take the oil of unction and anoint the tabernacle with its vessels, that they may be sanctified:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The altar of holocaust and all its vessels:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The laver with its foot: thou shalt consecrate all with the oil of unction, that they may be most holy.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And thou shalt bring Aaron and his sons to the door of the tabernacle of the testimony, and having washed them with water,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thou shalt put on them the holy vestments, that they may minister to me, and that the unction of them may prosper to an everlasting priesthood.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Moses did all that the Lord had commanded.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: So in the first month of the second year, the first day of the month, the tabernacle was set up.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Moses reared it up, and placed the boards and the sockets and the bars, and set up the pillars,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And spread the roof over the tabernacle, putting over it a cover, as the Lord had commanded.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he put the testimony in the ark, thrusting bars underneath, and the oracle above.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And when he had brought the ark into the tabernacle, he drew the veil before it to fulfil the commandment of the Lord.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he set the table in the tabernacle of the testimony, at the north side, without the veil,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Setting there in order the loaves of proposition, as the Lord had commanded Moses.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: He set the candlestick also in the tabernacle of the testimony, over against the table on the south side,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Placing the lamps in order, according to the precept of the Lord.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: He set also the altar of gold under the roof of the testimony, over against the veil,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And burnt upon it the incense of spices, as the Lord had commanded Moses.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he put also the hanging in the entry of the tabernacle of the testimony,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the altar of holocaust in the entry of the testimony, offering the holocaust, and the sacrifices upon it, as the Lord had commanded.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he set the laver between the tabernacle of the testimony and the altar, filling it with water.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Moses and Aaron, and his sons, washed their hands and feet,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: When they went into the tabernacle of the covenant, and went to the altar, as the Lord had commanded Moses.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: He set up also the court round about the tabernacle and the altar, drawing the hanging in the entry thereof. After all things were perfected,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The cloud covered the tabernacle of the testimony, and the glory of the Lord filled it.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Neither could Moses go into the tabernacle of the covenant, the cloud covering all things, and the majesty of the Lord shining, for the cloud had covered all.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: If at any time the cloud removed from the tabernacle, the children of Israel went forward by their troops:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: If it hung over, they remained in the same place.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For the cloud of the Lord hung over the tabernacle by day, and a fire by night, in the sight of all the children of Israel throughout all their mansions.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner
